jokes.text: Jason Alexander? Uh, only if we can't get Michael Richards or Jerry Seinfeld.
jokes.setup: Staff discussing booking Jason Alexander as a guest
jokes.punchline: Artie's casual dismissal ranks Alexander as third choice among Seinfeld cast
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Artie - industry veteran casually ranking sitcom stars. Economy and insider perspective make it work.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Jason Alexander? Uh, only if we can't get Michael Richards or Jerry Seinfeld.

jokes.text: Mary Lou retton? Marilu henner. Oh, thank God. The last thing we need is Larry on those uneven bars again.
jokes.setup: Mishearing guest name as Olympic gymnast
jokes.punchline: Relief followed by absurd image of Larry doing gymnastics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from simple mishearing to vivid absurd image. 'Again' suggests this has happened before, perfect Larry Sanders paranoia.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Mary Lou retton? Marilu henner. Oh, thank God. The last thing we need is Larry on those uneven bars again.

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh! Good one, Artie. Oh, I liked their first album, but the second one was a disappointment.
jokes.setup: Artie clarifies The Breeders are a rock band
jokes.punchline: Paula pretends she knew all along and offers fake music criticism
jokes.explanation: Classic character beat - Paula covering her ignorance with fake expertise. Well-executed but standard sitcom territory.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Oh, my gosh! Good one, Artie. Oh, I liked their first album, but the second one was a disappointment.

jokes.text: They got the stain completely out. I can't believe it. Well, we'll use them as our regular dry cleaner from now on.
jokes.setup: Larry examining what appears to be his clothing during a meeting
jokes.punchline: Completely oblivious to meeting, focused entirely on dry cleaning success
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders moment - narcissistic focus on trivial personal concerns during professional meeting. Character integration is flawless.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: They got the stain completely out. I can't believe it. Well, we'll use them as our regular dry cleaner from now on.

jokes.text: Are we ordering lunch? No, Hank. We're not. Oh, because I heard someone mention potatoes, and I... I can have those on my new diet.
jokes.setup: Hank bursts into booking meeting asking about lunch
jokes.punchline: His logic: potato lady = ordering potatoes for lunch
jokes.explanation: Pure Hank - self-absorbed misunderstanding combined with diet obsession. The logic leap from guest to lunch order is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Are we ordering lunch?

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: No, Hank. We're not.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, because I heard someone mention potatoes, and I... I can have those on my new diet.

jokes.text: Please do, because I only have the one shake in the morning.
jokes.setup: Hank being told he'll be kept advised about lunch
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary detail about his morning diet routine
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment - Hank's tendency to overshare personal details. Not groundbreaking but consistent with character.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Please do, because I only have the one shake in the morning.

jokes.text: His agent said he wouldn't be feeling well tonight. Yeah, I've had that.
jokes.setup: Danny DeVito canceling with flimsy excuse
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of the fake illness game
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - three words that perfectly capture Larry's understanding of show business BS. Industry insider humor.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: His agent said he wouldn't be feeling well tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah, I've had that.

jokes.text: Well, perhaps the fact that we pay them scale and send 'em home with a piece of shit t-shirt and hat...
jokes.setup: Paula explaining why guests cancel
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of their cheap guest treatment
jokes.explanation: Paula's frustrated honesty cuts through the BS. The phrase 'piece of shit t-shirt' is perfectly crude and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Well, perhaps the fact that we pay them scale and send 'em home with a piece of shit t-shirt and hat...

jokes.text: I really appreciate the effort that you put into your job each day.
jokes.setup: Larry addressing Paula's concerns about guest cancellations
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive corporate speak as veiled threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders passive-aggression. The politeness makes it more menacing. Sets up excellent callback later.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I really appreciate the effort that you put into your job each day.

jokes.text: Okay, what he said was: 'I really appreciate the effort you put into your job each day.' Ooh, girl, you are fucked.
jokes.setup: Paula recounting Larry's 'compliment' to Beverly
jokes.punchline: Beverly immediately recognizing it as a death sentence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms the earlier moment. Beverly's blunt 'you are fucked' is perfectly delivered and highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Okay, what he said was: 'I really appreciate the effort you put into your job each day.'

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: Ooh, girl, you are fucked.

jokes.text: I don't think she's got the right personality. I think she's too... abrasive? No, it's, uh... brusque? No. Incapable of putting a performer at ease because she's has a basic contempt for the business.
jokes.setup: Larry struggling to articulate Paula's problem
jokes.punchline: Escalates from simple adjectives to devastating character assassination
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure - Larry searching for the right word and landing on something much harsher than expected. Character-specific overthinking.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I don't think she's got the right personality. I think she's too... abrasive? No, it's, uh... brusque? No. Incapable of putting a performer at ease because she's has a basic contempt for the business.

jokes.text: And screw you, you glorified 10%-taking butt boy.
jokes.setup: Paula hanging up from trying to reach DeVito
jokes.punchline: Vicious insult directed at agent after polite conversation
jokes.explanation: Paula's frustration boiling over into creative profanity. '10%-taking butt boy' is a perfect industry-specific insult.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: And screw you, you glorified 10%-taking butt boy.

jokes.text: But it's in the shape of a hat? No, really, Larry, it's kind of embarrassing, because even the arsenio people gave out bathrobes with his big ol' triangle-shaped head on the back.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about the guest hat quality
jokes.punchline: Paula's vivid description of Arsenio's distinctive head shape on bathrobes
jokes.explanation: 'Big ol' triangle-shaped head' is a hilariously specific and slightly mean visual description that works perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: But it's in the shape of a hat?

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: No, really, Larry, it's kind of embarrassing, because even the arsenio people gave out bathrobes with his big ol' triangle-shaped head on the back.

jokes.text: even the arsenio people gave out bathrobes with his big ol' triangle-shaped head on the back
jokes.setup: Comparison to other talk shows' gifts
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of Arsenio's distinctive haircut on bathrobes
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke referencing Arsenio's distinctive look - specific and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: even the arsenio people gave out bathrobes with his big ol' triangle-shaped head on the back

jokes.text: Damn it, if this stage caves in, you won't need bathrobes. You'll need bodybags.
jokes.setup: Larry obsessing over guest gifts while Artie worries about earthquake damage
jokes.punchline: Artie's morbid escalation from bathrobes to bodybags
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - dark humor to cut through Larry's obsessive trivia. The alliteration of 'bathrobes to bodybags' makes it stick.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Damn it, if this stage caves in, you won't need bathrobes. You'll need bodybags.

jokes.text: Well, evidently, we already give a bag.
jokes.setup: Artie's bodybag comment
jokes.punchline: Larry completely missing the point, focusing on literal bag detail
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Larry's complete obliviousness to Artie's dark humor, focusing on the wrong 'bag.' Perfect economy and character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, evidently, we already give a bag.

jokes.text: Not if I kill myself before 5:30.
jokes.setup: Asked if lying about DeVito's return is a problem
jokes.punchline: Specific timeline for suicide as problem-solving
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with specific timing makes it funnier. Paula's desperation expressed through morbid precision.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Not if I kill myself before 5:30.

jokes.text: Or is there just my suit floating in space?
jokes.setup: Artie complaining that Larry doesn't see him
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of invisible Artie with floating suit
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy - the image of a floating suit is absurd and memorable. Perfectly captures Artie's frustration with being ignored.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Or is there just my suit floating in space?

jokes.text: Well, you know, those are made to be seen, not to be worn. They're made to hang in your closet so when someone looks in there they say, 'oh, you've been on arsenio.'
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on robe thickness
jokes.punchline: Phil explains bathrobes are trophies, not functional clothing
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about the reality of promotional gifts. Not brilliant but captures the absurdity of show business swag.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Well, you know, those are made to be seen, not to be worn. They're made to hang in your closet so when someone looks in there they say, 'oh, you've been on arsenio.'

jokes.text: Milk chocolate necktie. He does. Well, those are probably meant to be eaten, not worn.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what Leno gives as gifts
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact analysis of edible neckwear function
jokes.explanation: Absurd gift idea with Larry's typical overthinking of practical details. Decent but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Milk chocolate necktie. He does.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, those are probably meant to be eaten, not worn.

jokes.text: Is that suzanne pleshette? Phil, get out!
jokes.setup: Paula on phone trying to book DeVito
jokes.punchline: Phil's inappropriate interruption during desperate call
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment as Paula's desperation gets interrupted. Solid character work but not remarkable.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Is that suzanne pleshette? Phil, get out!

jokes.text: Although, you know, I'd feel a little awkward knowing that strangers were putting their lips on my head.
jokes.setup: Looking at Whoopi Goldberg mug as potential gift
jokes.punchline: Larry's neurotic concern about mug usage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders neurosis - turning a simple promotional mug into sexual anxiety. Character integration is flawless.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Although, you know, I'd feel a little awkward knowing that strangers were putting their lips on my head.

jokes.text: War of the roses is one of Larry's favorite films. Did I mention that?
jokes.setup: Paula desperately trying to convince DeVito to do the show
jokes.punchline: Pathetic attempt to find personal connection
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Paula grasping at straws. The randomness of the movie reference makes it appropriately desperate.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: War of the roses is one of Larry's favorite films. Did I mention that?

jokes.text: This is getting very sad. Do you have any shame at all?
jokes.setup: Paula's increasingly desperate booking attempts
jokes.punchline: DeVito directly calling out her shameless behavior
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on the desperate tactics of TV bookers

dialogue.speaker: Danny DeVito
dialogue.line: This is getting very sad. Do you have any shame at all?

jokes.text: I'm like a dog that way. I just have a very bad feeling.
jokes.setup: Hank claiming he can sense earthquakes coming
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to a dog with supernatural earthquake detection
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank self-aggrandizement through ridiculous comparison. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'm like a dog that way. I just have a very bad feeling.

jokes.text: No, that was Hank clearing his throat.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinks they felt an aftershock
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was just Hank's throat clearing
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection - builds earthquake tension then deflates with mundane explanation. Good structure and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: No, that was Hank clearing his throat.

jokes.text: Because twice today, he said that I was doing a really good job. Oh... I didn't know he said it twice.
jokes.setup: Paula worried Larry might fire her
jokes.punchline: Hank's reaction shows twice makes it even worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the corporate politeness = firing setup

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Because twice today, he said that I was doing a really good job.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh... I didn't know he said it twice.

jokes.text: You're digging a mighty deep hole over there. Good. Then I can jump in after I shoot myself in the head.
jokes.setup: Beverly warning Paula about her lies
jokes.punchline: Paula's morbid escalation from hole metaphor to suicide plan
jokes.explanation: Paula's desperation expressed through dark humor escalation. Works well with her character arc but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Beverly
dialogue.line: You're digging a mighty deep hole over there.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Good. Then I can jump in after I shoot myself in the head.

jokes.text: Just think of whatever you would ask a potato lady. And that's all there is to it? And put question marks at the end so Larry knows that they're questions.
jokes.setup: Paula delegating potato lady interview prep to Beverly
jokes.punchline: Condescending explanation of how question marks work
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - treating 'potato lady' as normal while being condescending about basic punctuation. Paula's desperation showing.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Just think of whatever you would ask a potato lady. And that's all there is to it? And put question marks at the end so Larry knows that they're questions.

jokes.text: And put question marks at the end so Larry knows that they're questions.
jokes.setup: Giving Beverly instructions for writing interview questions
jokes.punchline: Having to specify the most basic element of question format
jokes.explanation: Good workplace humor about having to over-explain simple tasks

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: And put question marks at the end so Larry knows that they're questions.

jokes.text: He's putting my photo on the inner thigh. Oh, God damn it. Maybe you're right. Maybe it should be the outer thigh.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing jogging suit customization while ignoring earthquake danger
jokes.punchline: Artie's exasperation followed by Larry's continued focus on thigh placement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders priorities - obsessing over photo placement while building collapses. The inner/outer thigh debate is perfectly narcissistic.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: He's putting my photo on the inner thigh. Oh, God damn it. Maybe you're right. Maybe it should be the outer thigh.

jokes.text: I don't want to talk to the fucking potato lady.
jokes.setup: Larry being told the potato lady questions are terrible
jokes.punchline: Explosive frustration with absurd guest booking
jokes.explanation: Larry's breaking point with the absurd guest booking. The profanity makes the mundane 'potato lady' funnier through contrast.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I don't want to talk to the fucking potato lady.

jokes.text: Um, I think it's just tremendous what you're doing here for juvenile diabetes. And I don't know if you know this, but Larry has low-blood sugar. It's not the same. Is it? It's not the same.
jokes.setup: Paula desperately trying to connect with DeVito through medical conditions
jokes.punchline: DeVito's flat rejection of her false equivalency
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Paula's desperate attempt to find connection immediately shut down. The repetition of 'It's not the same' drives home the failure.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Um, I think it's just tremendous what you're doing here for juvenile diabetes. And I don't know if you know this, but Larry has low-blood sugar.

dialogue.speaker: DeVito
dialogue.line: It's not the same. Is it? It's not the same.

jokes.text: Are you telling me that you can't walk 200 feet to plug your own movie? It'll be great exposure, and it'll prevent Larry from firing me. Please?
jokes.setup: Paula's final desperate pitch to DeVito
jokes.punchline: Combining professional appeal with personal desperation
jokes.explanation: Paula's complete breakdown of professional boundaries. The specific '200 feet' makes it more pathetic and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Are you telling me that you can't walk 200 feet to plug your own movie? It'll be great exposure, and it'll prevent Larry from firing me. Please?

jokes.text: Iook at this. The name of the horse... Larry's valentine. Jockey... Hank Espinoza. The trainer's name is Bill. Bill, just like the guy who writes for us. Phil. No, it's Bill. See...
jokes.setup: Artie trying to convince Larry to bet on horse based on coincidences
jokes.punchline: Larry correcting the wrong name while missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Artie's gambling superstition vs Larry's pedantic corrections. Larry missing the forest for the trees.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Iook at this. The name of the horse... Larry's valentine. Jockey... Hank Espinoza. The trainer's name is Bill. Bill, just like the guy who writes for us. Phil. No, it's Bill. See...

jokes.punchline: The absurd coincidences that Artie sees as meaningful
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Artie's gambling superstitions and pattern-seeking

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: The name of the horse... Larry's valentine. Jockey... Hank Espinoza. The trainer's name is Bill.

jokes.text: Guy robs a lady in the subway. The cops come to try to stop this guy. The criminal refuses to stop. They shoot the guy in the back. Paralyze the criminal. The criminal sues the transit system for $4.5 million... and he won the case. Now he's suing nike, 'cause he says if he had had better sneakers, he'd been able to run faster, and they never would have caught him.
jokes.setup: Jimmie Walker doing stand-up about crime and lawsuits
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation to suing Nike for inadequate crime footwear
jokes.explanation: Solid stand-up routine with good escalation. The Nike punchline is absurd and well-constructed but this is Walker's stand-up, not sitcom.

dialogue.speaker: Jimmie Walker
dialogue.line: Guy robs a lady in the subway. The cops come to try to stop this guy. The criminal refuses to stop. They shoot the guy in the back. Paralyze the criminal. The criminal sues the transit system for $4.5 million... and he won the case. Now he's suing nike, 'cause he says if he had had better sneakers, he'd been able to run faster, and they never would have caught him.

jokes.text: Where is he? He's in make-up. One more segment with the potato lady, and then Danny... I'm not doing a dumb segment with the potato lady.
jokes.setup: Paula lying about DeVito's presence
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate rejection of potato lady segment
jokes.explanation: Larry's continued disdain for the potato lady, showing his limits for absurd guests. Character-consistent but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Where is he? He's in make-up.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: One more segment with the potato lady, and then Danny...

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I'm not doing a dumb segment with the potato lady.

jokes.text: I got to tell you the truth. I'm torn but, uh... madison is who it looks like to me. I would take another guess if I were you.
jokes.setup: Larry interviewing potato lady about potato resembling presidents
jokes.punchline: Larry's polite but condescending rejection of her identification
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Larry trying to be polite while clearly thinking the guest is ridiculous. His diplomatic condescension is character-perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I got to tell you the truth. I'm torn but, uh... madison is who it looks like to me. I would take another guess if I were you.

jokes.text: I never had the bad feeling worse than I do right now.
jokes.setup: Another earthquake prediction from Hank
jokes.punchline: Escalating his previous 'dog-like' earthquake sense claims
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation but not adding much new. Hank's character consistency but lacks originality.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I never had the bad feeling worse than I do right now.

jokes.text: Late? I just decided 5 minutes ago to do the show. What do you mean late?
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on DeVito being late
jokes.punchline: DeVito revealing he just agreed to appear moments ago
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Larry's misconception about timing vs reality. DeVito's matter-of-fact correction is perfectly delivered.

dialogue.speaker: DeVito
dialogue.line: Late? I just decided 5 minutes ago to do the show. What do you mean late?

jokes.text: Well, your booker, she said that you were going to fire her if... if I didn't, uh, show up. I don't want that on my conscious. She's troubled.
jokes.setup: DeVito explaining why he decided to do show
jokes.punchline: Casual assessment of Paula as 'troubled'
jokes.explanation: DeVito's blunt but compassionate assessment. 'She's troubled' is delivered with perfect deadpan concern, making Paula's desperation into a diagnosis.

dialogue.speaker: DeVito
dialogue.line: Well, your booker, she said that you were going to fire her if... if I didn't, uh, show up. I don't want that on my conscious. She's troubled.

jokes.text: your booker, she said that you were going to fire her if... if I didn't, uh, show up. I don't want that on my conscious.
jokes.setup: Larry asking why DeVito decided to do the show
jokes.punchline: DeVito reveals Paula's desperate tactics worked
jokes.explanation: Good callback revealing Paula's desperate strategy actually worked

dialogue.speaker: Danny DeVito
dialogue.line: your booker, she said that you were going to fire her if... if I didn't, uh, show up. I don't want that on my conscious.

jokes.text: Now I look like one of those pussies who got under the desk.
jokes.setup: Hank's reaction to earthquake scare on TV
jokes.punchline: His concern about looking cowardly on national television
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank - more worried about his image than actual safety. The specific 'under the desk' reference makes it more vivid.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Now I look like one of those pussies who got under the desk.

jokes.text: Hot to trot. OK, I knew it. Breathe.

jokes.punchline: Treating Hank's panic as psychic gambling advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Artie exploiting Hank's earthquake panic for gambling tips. The matter-of-fact 'I knew it' followed by 'breathe' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Hot to trot. OK, I knew it. Breathe.

jokes.text: if I'm gonna die, I want to die with my family, not with jimmie walker and the weirdo with the doughnuts. Potatoes? The potato lady? Whatever.

jokes.punchline: His dismissive categorization of the other guests
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from noble family sentiment to brutal dismissal of fellow guests. 'Weirdo with the doughnuts' is perfectly dismissive and quotable.

dialogue.line: if I'm gonna die, I want to die with my family, not with jimmie walker and the weirdo with the doughnuts. Potatoes? The potato lady? Whatever.

dialogue.line: Potatoes? The potato lady? Whatever.

jokes.text: Oh, hell, yes. We could put your cock on it. As long as it's not the hour hand.

jokes.punchline: Phil's crude escalation with specific logistical concern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from innocent customization to crude suggestion with perfect practical concern. 'As long as it's not the hour hand' is comedy gold.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Oh, hell, yes. We could put your cock on it. As long as it's not the hour hand.

jokes.text: You wouldn't believe that lengths I have to go to. The other week, when Tom hanks tried to back out, I had to cry and feign illness.
jokes.setup: Paula defending her tactics to Larry
jokes.punchline: Revealing even more desperate methods used on A-list stars
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation - Paula's methods get more desperate with bigger stars. Tom Hanks requiring fake illness is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: You wouldn't believe that lengths I have to go to. The other week, when Tom hanks tried to back out, I had to cry and feign illness.
